cases.name: memory write with explicit provenance edges
cases.current_v1_1_expression: Do[Create["Entity", {"id":"memory:handoff-2026-05-05", "kind":"agent_memory", "tier":"handoff", "summary":"parser pressure test ready"}], Create["Edge", {"from":"event:session-42", "to":"memory:handoff-2026-05-05", "relation_type":"captured_in"}], Create["Edge", {"from":"agent:planner", "to":"memory:handoff-2026-05-05", "relation_type":"authored"}]]

expected.canonical_expression: Do[Create["Entity", {"id": "memory:handoff-2026-05-05", "kind": "agent_memory", "summary": "parser pressure test ready", "tier": "handoff"}], Create["Edge", {"from": "event:session-42", "relation_type": "captured_in", "to": "memory:handoff-2026-05-05"}], Create["Edge", {"from": "agent:planner", "relation_type": "authored", "to": "memory:handoff-2026-05-05"}]]

cases.pressure_note: A basic memory write repeats the same provenance edge pattern as other durable output writes.
cases.promotion_risk: A memory-specific helper must not turn CogLang into a memory store or collapse host audit metadata into in-graph provenance.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a strict memory provenance profile, StoreMemoryWithProvenance should expand to a memory node plus captured_in/authored edges and preserve write-review preflight posture.

cases.name: recall with read-set tracking pressure
cases.pressure_note: Current v1.1.0 can trace a recall expression, but it does not produce a host-level read-set token for later stale-read checks.
cases.promotion_risk: Read-set tracking likely belongs first to host execution/HRC evidence, not core language semantics.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a recall query, RecallWithReadSet should preserve the query result while returning a deterministic read_set_id or trace field that a later guarded write can cite.

cases.name: embedding backfill transform pressure
cases.pressure_note: Bulk memory maintenance workflows are expressible with ForEach + Query + Update, but dry-run and batch bounds are host-policy concepts hidden in private convention.
cases.promotion_risk: Transform helpers should start as profile integration; otherwise CogLang risks becoming an operational job scheduler.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a bounded selector, MemoryTransform should preflight aggregate read/write cost, require graph.write, and expose dry_run as a host-visible policy field.

cases.name: retention cleanup pressure
cases.pressure_note: Retention workflows need explicit max-delete and soft-delete posture; current expressions make those bounds a private host convention.
cases.promotion_risk: Retention policy is domain-specific and should not enter core without repeated evidence across non-memory applications.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given an expired-memory selector, RetentionCleanup should require review, expose max_deletes in preflight, and never imply hard deletion.

cases.name: stale-read guarded update pressure
cases.pressure_note: Stale-read protection is possible as ordinary expression logic, but the host cannot see an atomic compare-and-write intent.
cases.promotion_risk: Guarded writes belong on the HRC evolution path unless a purely expression-level form can preserve atomicity without host guarantees.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a previous read_set or revision token, GuardedUpdate should preflight as read+write, require review, and return a deterministic stale_read error if the guard fails.
